steps.title: Identify the elements in the phrase
steps.content: The blank must include two pieces of information about the author: the role (poet) and the name (Emily Hiestand). These are followed by another descriptive element in the sentence: author of The Very Rich Hours.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for identifying names vs. additional details
steps.content:
When a name identifies which specific person you're describing, it should not be set off by commas — for example, the poet Emily Hiestand without commas around the name.

When a descriptive phrase provides additional information (like author of...), it must be separated by a comma: the poet Emily Hiestand, author of The Very Rich Hours.

The other choices either incorrectly place commas around the name or fail to separate the descriptive phrase.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
